content: Any knows how to open a socket to remote server ? I can not find TcpStream anywhere :thinking:
You mean from a kernel driver? Or a hostprogs tool?
Ah, we don't have those abstractions yet
However somebody did start to work on some abstractions in that area (cc @Valentin Obst) https://github.com/Rust-for-Linux/linux/pull/1062, maybe that has part of what you need?

it is still in draft mode :/
Is there a way to use smolctp crate while waiting for the PR to be merged ?
https://github.com/smoltcp-rs/smoltcp
is not, what are my options ?
A large portion of the kernel’s API surface is in draft mode when it comes to rust support :)
The kernel has its own tcp interfaces, see e.g. https://linux-kernel-labs.github.io/refs/heads/master/labs/networking.html. You can use the C interfaces unsafely just for experimentation
But anything that gets merged we need good safe abstractions for, like in that draft PR. What is your use case?
my use case is to build a module to report various information to a remote server, just a side project to get into the kernel development nothing fancy. but I got stuck before even starting :D
